Impressive if it can get the specific viewpoint it's taken from. I don't remember which it is, but one of the southern ones from atop a hill. Daytime, summer, clear weather.

details.description: Elevated panoramic view over Florence under a clear blue sky. In the middle distance the city’s dense terracotta rooftops stretch across the valley, dominated by the Cathedral of Santa Maria del Fiore: its large brick-red dome (Brunelleschi’s Cupola) and the white, polychrome-marble Giotto’s Campanile are clearly visible. To the left a smaller domed mass, likely the Medici Chapels of San Lorenzo, rises above surrounding roofs. A slender Gothic spire (Badia Fiorentina) is also discernible. In the foreground are elements of a historic hillside cemetery—stone retaining walls, carved grave slabs, hedges and cypress trees—consistent with the setting beside San Miniato al Monte. Beyond the city, blue-green Tuscan hills form the backdrop. The light suggests late afternoon, with crisp contrast and long shadows.

details.description: A panoramic Florence cityscape viewed from a hillside vantage. In the foreground, a low stone wall, a modern street lamp, and a carved memorial plaque are framed by trimmed greenery and slender cypress trees, suggesting a cultivated vantage point or garden. Beyond, the dense urban fabric of Florence unfolds with the iconic Florence Cathedral (Duomo) domed roof dominating the skyline, flanked by Giotto's Campanile to the left and the Baptistery nearby. The distant hills form a blue backdrop under a clear, bright sky, with sharp daylight creating distinct shadows and a crisp horizon line.

entities.name: Cathedral of Santa Maria del Fiore (Florence Cathedral)
entities.notes: Gothic-Renaissance cathedral; dome engineered by Filippo Brunelleschi; construction 1296–1436; central feature of Florence's skyline.

entities.name: Giotto's Campanile
entities.notes: Bell tower of the Florence Cathedral; Gothic architecture; built 1334–1359.

entities.name: Battistero di San Giovanni (Baptistery of St. John)
entities.notes: Octagonal baptistery adjacent to the cathedral; renowned for its decorative bronze doors and mosaics.

entities.name: Basilica di Santa Croce
entities.notes: Major Gothic church in Florence; prominent landmark visible in long-range city skylines from certain vantage points.

details.description: Elevated panoramic view across Florence, Italy, centered on the Cathedral of Santa Maria del Fiore (Duomo) with its red‑tiled Brunelleschi dome and the adjacent white‑marble Giotto’s Campanile. Terracotta rooftops of the historic center spread below, with additional church domes and a slender spired bell tower to the left. In the foreground are elements of a hillside cemetery—carved headstones, a stone retaining wall, hedges, a light post, and tall cypress trees—consistent with the Cimitero delle Porte Sante by San Miniato al Monte. The scene is lit by warm late‑afternoon light with layered blue hills under a clear sky.

details.description: A panoramic Florence cityscape viewed from a hillside cemetery area, with a rough stone wall and tomb in the foreground and a modern street lamp toward the center. Foreground vegetation includes manicured hedges and tall cypress-like trees. Beyond, Florence unfolds with dense red-tiled roofs, culminating in the Florence Cathedral (Santa Maria del Fiore) dominating the skyline, its distinctive Brunelleschi-designed red-brick dome flanked by Giotto’s Campanile. Distant hills backdrop a clear blue sky. The vantage is consistent with positions near Porta delle Porte Sante or San Miniato al Monte.

details.description: A vertical, high-angle shot capturing a panoramic view of Florence, Italy, framed by the foreground elements of a cemetery. In the immediate foreground, lush green bushes and trees partially obscure a stone wall and several grave markers, including a prominent vertical stone slab with intricate carvings, indicating the photographer is standing within the Cimitero delle Porte Sante or the grounds of San Miniato al Monte. A modern metal pole with a security camera or light fixture stands in the center foreground. The midground is dominated by the dense, terracotta-roofed cityscape of Florence. The most recognizable landmarks are the massive, red-tiled dome of the Cathedral of Santa Maria del Fiore (the Duomo) and the slender, polychrome marble Giotto's Campanile immediately to its left. Further to the left, the smaller red dome of the Cappelle Medicee (Medici Chapels) is visible. The background features the rolling blue hills and mountains of the Tuscan landscape under a bright blue sky with wispy white clouds.

details.description: A high-angle panoramic view of Florence, Italy, taken from an elevated vantage point. The skyline is dominated by the Cathedral of Santa Maria del Fiore with Brunelleschi's red-tiled dome and Giotto's Campanile. To the left, the tower of Palazzo Vecchio and the dome of the Medici Chapel (San Lorenzo) are visible. The foreground features a stone wall, cypress trees, and elements of a cemetery, including a carved headstone. In the background, the rolling hills of the Tuscan landscape rise under a clear blue sky with light clouds.

details.description: Panoramic view over Florence with the city spread across a valley and the Apennine/Tuscan hills beyond under a clear blue sky. Dominating the center is the Florence Cathedral (Santa Maria del Fiore) with Brunelleschi’s large terracotta dome and the adjacent white‑green‑pink marble Giotto’s Campanile. Dense historic roofs surround the cathedral. In the foreground are stone terrace walls, a carved funerary slab, trimmed hedges, cypress trees, and a modern lamp post—elements consistent with the hillside grounds and cemetery near San Miniato al Monte above the city.

details.description: A sunlit panoramic view of Florence, Italy, captured from a hillside terrace. In the foreground, a stone wall and carved panel flank green shrubbery and a paved terrace. The city sprawls below with medieval rooftops and towers, dominated by the red-brick dome of the Florence Cathedral (Santa Maria del Fiore) rising prominently in the middle distance, topped by a lantern. The backdrop features rolling hills and a clear blue sky with a few wispy clouds. The overall scene combines historic urban fabric with a sense of elevation from the vantage point.

details.description: Elevated view across Florence, Italy, looking over a hillside terrace with stone cemetery walls and plaques, manicured hedges, cypress trees, and a modern streetlamp in the foreground. Dominating the skyline at center is the terracotta-tiled dome and lantern of the Cathedral of Santa Maria del Fiore (the Duomo), with Giotto’s marble-clad campanile rising beside it. The scene spans a dense expanse of ochre and red-roofed buildings in the historic center, set against layered blue Tuscan hills under a clear sky. The vantage appears to be from the hillside south of the Arno near San Miniato al Monte/Piazzale Michelangelo.

details.description: An elevated vantage point on a stone terrace overlooking Florence, Italy. In the foreground is a carved rectangular stone tablet set into a low wall, with hedges and dark stone walls and evergreen trees along the edge of the viewpoint. Beyond, the dense city of Florence stretches across rolling hills: terracotta rooftops, church spires, and historic buildings. The centerpiece is the Florence Cathedral (Santa Maria del Fiore) with its large red-tiled dome and white marble accents, flanked by bell towers and additional church spires along the skyline. In the distance, hazy hills and mountains rise to meet a bright blue sky with a few wispy clouds. The lighting is bright and high-contrast, suggesting midday sun.

entities.name: Florence Cathedral (Santa Maria del Fiore)
entities.notes: Cathedral with iconic red-tiled dome by Brunelleschi

details.description: A panoramic view of the city of Florence, Italy, captured from an elevated vantage point and currently rotated 90 degrees clockwise. When oriented correctly, the foreground features a stone wall and a decorative tombstone with intricate carvings, surrounded by green foliage, identifying the location as the Cimitero delle Porte Sante (Sacred Doors Cemetery). The cityscape is dominated by the iconic Cathedral of Santa Maria del Fiore (the Duomo) with Brunelleschi's massive terracotta dome and the adjacent Giotto's Campanile. To the left of the cathedral, the tower of the Palazzo Vecchio (Torre di Arnolfo) rises above the sea of red-tiled roofs. In the background, hazy blue mountains frame the city under a bright sky with wispy clouds.

details.description: A vertical panoramic view of Florence, Italy, captured from an elevated vantage point. The central focus is the historic city center, dominated by the Cattedrale di Santa Maria del Fiore with its massive terracotta-colored dome (Brunelleschi's Dome) and the slender, white marble Giotto's Campanile. The dense urban landscape of red-tiled roofs fills the valley, leading toward the hazy blue silhouettes of the Apennine Mountains in the distance. In the immediate foreground, a weathered stone wall and lush green cypress trees frame the scene. A portion of a stone monument with intricate carvings is visible on the right, indicating the photographer was likely standing in a cemetery or a monumental terrace. The sky is clear blue with a few wispy clouds, and the lighting suggests a bright, sunny day.
